extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property is VIOLATED because rule r3 (leg_swelling -> self_care) carries priority 100, which dominates every other rule (all priority <= 3). Whenever leg_swelling is true, r3 wins the conflict and forces acuity to self_care, the lowest acuity level.

Concrete counterexample (the 'after' state, with its matching 'before'):
- BEFORE: leg_swelling=false, fever=true, confusion=false, vision_loss=false. Only r1 matches (priority 3) -> acuity = emergent (highest).
- AFTER (set leg_swelling=true, all else unchanged): leg_swelling=true, fever=true, confusion=false, vision_loss=false. Matching rules are r1 (priority 3 -> emergent) and r3 (priority 100 -> self_care). The highest-priority matching rule wins per the conflict-resolution policy, so r3 fires and acuity = self_care.

Acuity drops from emergent to self_care when leg_swelling is turned on, which is strictly lower in the listed enum order (self_care < routine < urgent < emergent). This directly contradicts the plain-language intent that setting leg_swelling true never lowers acuity. The witness_assignment given is the AFTER input where the lowered acuity (self_care) occurs; the unchanged comparison input differs only in leg_swelling=false and yields emergent.
